items.title: Oil climbs after renewed US, Iran strikes in Middle East
items.desc: Oil prices rose today following days of tit-for-tat strikes by the US and Iran that underscored the fragility of their interim peace deal and again slowed energy shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: Hostilities halted again as peace talks continue
items.desc: The U.S. and Iran agree to pause fresh hostilities, sending futures higher. But concerns over the peace talks continue, pushing oil back above $70 a barrel.

items.title: Bitcoin dips to $59,700 as Iran de-escalation lifts stocks but not crypto
items.desc: U.S. equity futures rose after reports the U.S. and Iran agreed to halt strikes and resume talks. Bitcoin has barely moved, still down 6.8% on the week.
